hypothesis: Augmenting the multi-agent framework with a persistent semantic-episodic memory encoding validated simulation heuristics (e.g., correct Widom insertion settings, standard 12 Å cutoffs, and mandatory structure-file propagation) will raise the success rate of automated single-adsorbate heat-of-adsorption setups across 500 zeolite structures from 80% to at least 95% without reducing the current 100% execution rate.
